Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze a provided event log of customer support tickets to identify cases with long resolution times determine the root causes of these delays and provide recommendations for improvement

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data I need to process the event log table row by row grouping by Case ID to calculate total duration and analyze specific activity gaps

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 101 Start 2024 03 01 08 00 End 2024 03 01 10 15 Total Duration 2 hours 15 minutes Activities Receive to Triage 10m to Assign 10m to Investigate 40m to Resolve 1h to Close 15m Escalation No Status Fast

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 Start 2024 03 01 08 05 End 2024 03 02 09 15 Total Duration 25 hours 10 minutes Activities Receive to Triage 25m to Assign 30m to Escalate 2 point 5h wait to Investigate 2 point 5h to Wait overnight 19h to Resolve 15m to Close Escalation Yes to Level 2 Status Slow

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 103 Start 2024 03 01 08 10 End 2024 03 01 09 30 Total Duration 1 hour 20 minutes Activities Receive to Triage 5m to Assign 15m to Investigate 15m to Resolve 30m to Close 15m Escalation No Status Fastest

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 Start 2024 03 01 08 20 End 2024 03 02 08 30 Total Duration 24 hours 10 minutes Activities Receive to Triage 40m wait to Assign 30m wait to Wait 3 point 5h to Investigate 19h wait to Resolve 30m to Close Escalation No Status Slow

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 Start 2024 03 01 08 25 End 2024 03 03 09 30 Total Duration 49 hours 5 minutes Activities Receive to Triage 10m to Assign 25m to Investigate 10m to Escalate wait 28h to Investigate 19h to Resolve 15m to Close Escalation Yes to Level 2 Status Slowest

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Compare durations 101 2 point 25 hours 102 25 hours 103 1 point 33 hours 104 24 hours 105 49 hours Conclusion Cases 102 104 and 105 are significantly longer than 101 and 103

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 Escalated to Level 2 There is a massive gap between Investigate Issue and Resolve Ticket overnight Also the initial triage assign took longer than average

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 No escalation but still slow Look at the Receive to Triage gap and Assign to Investigate gap The biggest delay is between Investigate Issue and Resolve Ticket This is an overnight gap

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 Escalated This is the worst case Gap 1 Investigate Issue to Escalate is short Gap 2 Escalate to Investigate Issue is a 28 hour wait for the Level 2 team to pick it up Gap 3 Investigate Issue to Resolve is another overnight gap

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Common Patterns Escalations Cases 102 and 105 involve escalation Both are long Overnight Gaps All slow cases 102 104 105 have significant gaps spanning across days This suggests work stops after business hours or handoffs are delayed until the next morning Level 1 Bottleneck Case 104 did not escalate but waited 3 point 5 hours between assignment and investigation Level 2 Bottleneck Case 102 and 105 show massive wait times after escalation

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Factor 1 Escalation Process Escalated tickets take 2 to 4 times longer Why Waiting for Level 2 availability Factor 2 Business Hours Shift Work The overnight gaps suggest tickets are not being worked on after business hours Factor 3 Triage Assignment Latency Case 104 took 40 mins to triage Case 102 took 25 mins Case 103 took 5 mins Inconsistency here

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Recommendations Improve Level 2 capacity or service levels Implement 24 7 support or on call rotation for critical tickets Standardize triage times Analyze why tickets need escalation can Level 1 be trained better

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refinement on Calculations 101 08 00 to 10 15 2h 15m 102 08 05 to 09 15 next day 25h 10m 103 08 10 to 09 30 1h 20m 104 08 20 to 08 30 next day 24h 10m 105 08 25 to 09 30 Day 3 49h 5m Average of fast cases 101 103 1h 50m Average of slow cases 102 104 105 33 hours Comparison Slow cases are roughly 18 times slower than fast cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Specific Bottlenecks Escalation Wait Time 105 waited 28 hours for Level 2 to investigate 102 waited 19 hours between Investigation and Resolution Assignment Latency 104 waited 3 point 5 hours between Assignment and Investigation Triage Latency 104 took 40 mins to triage

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review Does this answer all parts of the prompt Identify long cases Yes 102 104 105 Root causes Yes Escalations Overnight gaps Level 2 backlog Recommendations Yes Capacity service levels Training

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait one detail check Case 104 does not have an escalation but is still slow I must ensure I highlight that not all slow cases are due to escalation 104 is a Level 1 ticket that sat idle This is a crucial distinction

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: It implies Level 1 agents might be overloaded or prioritizing other work OR the ticket was complex but did not trigger escalation rules

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Re evaluating Case 104 Receive 08 20 to Triage 09 00 40m wait Triage 09 00 to Assign 09 30 30m wait Assign 09 30 to Investigate 13 00 3 point 5h wait Investigate 13 00 Day 1 to Resolve 08 00 Day 2 19h wait This looks like a resource constraint issue plus business hours issue Re evaluating Case 105 Escalate 10 00 Day 1 to Investigate 14 00 Day 2 28h wait This is the biggest single gap in the dataset
